column1: # PURPOSE: Synthetic node (channel) data for automated testing.
# This file mimics the real "vis_channel_stats.csv" from the Recfluence dataset.
# Each row represents one fake YouTube channel that will become a NODE in the
# COLUMN DEFINITIONS (matching the real data exactly):
# CHANNEL_ID          — A unique identifier for the channel. In real data this
#                       is a YouTube channel ID like "UCxxxxxx". Here we use
#                       short readable IDs like "ch_L1" so tests are easy to
# CHANNEL_TITLE       — Human-readable name. Purely descriptive; the code
#                       easier when you can see "Left News 1" instead of
# CHANNEL_VIEWS       — Total lifetime views for the channel. Included to
#                       match the real schema. Not used in MVP analysis.
# COUNTRY             — Channel's country. All set to "US" to match the
#                       US-politics focus of the real dataset.
# RELEVANCE           — Float 0–1 indicating what fraction of the channel's
#                       content is relevant to US politics. We set most to 1.0
#                       (fully relevant) for simplicity. The isolated node
#                       gets 0.2 to test low-relevance handling later.
# SUBS                — Subscriber count. Included to match schema.
#                     — Average daily views over the channel's lifetime.
#                     — Same as above but only counting politically relevant
# CHANNEL_VIDEO_VIEWS — Total views across all videos. Slightly different
#                       from CHANNEL_VIEWS in the real data.
#                       (how often this channel's videos appear as
#                     — Average daily views on politically relevant videos.
# AVG_MINUTES         — Average video length in minutes.
# FROM_DATE           — Start of the data collection period for this channel.
# TO_DATE             — End of the data collection period.
#                         L = Left    → will be coded as -1
#                         C = Center  → will be coded as  0
#                         R = Right   → will be coded as +1
#                       This is what drives the entire drift analysis.
# TAGS                — JSON array of category tags. We use simple tags here.
#                       "MissingLinkMedia"). Not used in MVP but present
# MEDIA               — Media type. "YouTube" for independent creators
# LOGO_URL            — URL to the channel's logo image. Not used in analysis.
# 4. One isolated node (ch_island) with no edges → verify code handles
# 5. Total: 10 nodes → easy to verify by hand
